response.justification: Strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the role is fundamentally a C++ specialist position and the candidate has only ~6 months of professional C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014-2015) plus a university thesis. Primary expertise is JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web/full-stack work. There is no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL practices, industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems. Transferable skills (multi-threading via DLang, networking, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby) are present, but the core technical and security requirements are not met.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience with senior-level ownership and mentoring
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of req/s)
Strong testing and craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, OSS contributions
concerns: C++/STL professional experience is minimal (~6 months) and over a decade old
No demonstrated background in IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity
Missing IoT/industrial-automation domain exposure: no MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or message-broker C++ work

key_factors.factor: No IEC 62443 / SDL / industrial cybersecurity background
Strong general senior engineering and distributed-systems track record
